q: Why is herd immunity important in preventing epidemics?
options: When all individuals in a population are immune, pathogens evolve into less harmful strains
When enough individuals are immune, transmission chains break down and susceptible individuals (who cannot be vaccinated) are protected by reduced pathogen circulation
Herd immunity means everyone in the herd has the same genetic resistance to disease
A high proportion of immune individuals floods the environment with antibodies, neutralizing free-floating pathogens
explanation: When vaccination rates exceed the herd immunity threshold, the pathogen cannot find enough susceptible hosts to sustain spread, protecting immunocompromised or unvaccinated individuals indirectly.
